Part 4 Volatile Organic Compounds are reported on an as-carbon basis.  We have used information provided by the National Council for Air and Stream Improvement (NCASI) to calculate these numbers and believe that this is the current best available data for

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Sold more rough green material that does not require kiln drying - outcome less natural gas consumption

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Less product flowing through our Kiln Drying process - product shipped prior to drying, therefore used less Natrual Gas over the course of the year

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: Reduced production though Kiln drying and planermill

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: All VOCs that were released to the air in a quantity greater than or equal to 1 tonne have been speciated and reported in Part 5 as required.  There were some VOC’s released in quantities that were less than 1 tonne and as instructed, these were not reported in Part 5.  This is why there is a difference between the totals for Part 4 and Part 5 substances.
